title: NIfTI Phantom Registry
description: Index of public NIfTI phantoms: a top-level array of collections. Each collection points at one Zenodo record (by version DOI) holding one or more phantom JSON files and the NIfTI files they reference. The registry only references data; it does not store it. Collections allow additional properties so the format can evolve in place. See REGISTRY.md.

collection.description: A registered Zenodo record and the phantom files it contains. The collection name is unique across the registry and namespaces those files in references ('<collection>/<file>').
Unique collection name; identifies the entry and namespaces its phantom files in references (e.g. 'mrx-brain-cohort' -> 'mrx-brain-cohort/subj42-3T.json'). Must be unique across the registry.

description.description: One or two sentences describing the collection.

license.description: Reuse terms as an SPDX license identifier (e.g. 'CC-BY-4.0', 'CC0-1.0').

doi.description: Immutable Zenodo version DOI of the exact, byte-identical record version this collection currently points to (e.g. '10.5281/zenodo.1234568'). The DOI embeds the Zenodo record id ('zenodo.<id>'), so it alone is enough to resolve and download the files; no provider or URL is stored. The entry is mutable: it is updated in place to a new version DOI when the data is revised. To pin an exact resolution, reference '<collection>/<file>' at a specific nifti-phantoms commit.

phantoms.description: The phantom JSON filenames inside the Zenodo record (one per configuration, e.g. one per field strength). Each is referenced as '<collection>/<filename>'; the NIfTI files a phantom needs live in the same record.

email.description: Optional contact email for the author.
